meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no version control (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Mastering SaaS Pricing & Version Control for Students
content.seo_description: Learn to implement robust SaaS pricing and professional version control. A beginner-friendly guide using Antigravity and Claude Code for your first app.
content.answer_block: To solve the lack of version control while launching SaaS pricing, integrate Git-based workflows with automated billing logic. By using Antigravity to scaffold your architecture and Claude Code to handle Stripe API integration, you ensure every pricing change is tracked, reversible, and production-ready from your very first commit.
content.intro:
You are staring at a screen of chaotic, unversioned code—a digital house of cards waiting to collapse. Every tweak to your pricing logic feels like a gamble because you have no safety net to revert to. This is the 'no-man's land' where most student projects die, paralyzed by the fear that one wrong line of code will break the entire system.

But here is the secret: professional engineering isn't about writing perfect code; it's about building systems that withstand iteration. By marrying version control with a modular pricing architecture, you stop being a student and start being an architect. Let’s turn that chaos into a professional-grade SaaS engine.

steps.title: Initialize Versioned Foundation
steps.description: Establish a Git repository immediately to ensure your pricing logic is tracked before you write a single line of business code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project structure with Git tracking enabled. Set up a .gitignore file that excludes sensitive environment variables and dependencies.
steps.claude_code_command: git init && touch .gitignore && claude commit -m 'Initial project structure'

steps.title: Architecting the Pricing Model
steps.description: Define your SaaS pricing tiers as clean, modular data structures rather than hardcoded logic to allow for easy updates.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a constants.ts file defining three SaaS pricing tiers: Hobby, Pro, and Enterprise, including features and price points.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create constants.ts with tiered pricing objects'

steps.title: Integrating Stripe API
steps.description: Use Claude Code to securely map your pricing constants to Stripe Checkout sessions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a server-side handler for Stripe Checkout that maps the Pro tier constant to a specific Stripe Price ID.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement Stripe Checkout handler mapping constants to price IDs'

steps.title: Version-Controlled Iteration
steps.description: Every time you adjust a price, create a new branch to test the impact before merging.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Explain how to create a feature branch for a pricing experiment and run a diff check against the main branch.

steps.title: Automated Deployment Pipeline
steps.description: Connect your repository to a deployment platform to ensure your versioned code is always the live version.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a configuration script for a CI/CD pipeline that runs tests on every push to the main branch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a CI pipeline config for Vercel/GitHub Actions'

pro_tips: Never hardcode Stripe API keys; always reference them via process.env and verify their existence on app startup.
Use Semantic Versioning (SemVer) for your releases, even if you are the only user; it builds the habit of professional software release management.
Implement 'Feature Flags' for pricing changes so you can toggle between billing models without deploying new code.
Write 'Integration Tests' for your checkout flow; code that isn't tested is code that is already broken.
Commit messages should follow the Conventional Commits specification (e.g., 'feat: add enterprise pricing') to keep your history audit-ready for future employers.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing is live and versioned, focus on building a user dashboard that displays the specific features of their current plan based on the Stripe customer object.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation to scale your SaaS without the fear of breaking your codebase. You are moving from a hobbyist to a professional developer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
